# monaco-editor — Scala.js test-time stub

This directory is **not** a real install of the `monaco-editor` npm package. It
exists only so the Scala.js Node-based test runner for `krueger.webapp` can
resolve the ES-module import that Scala.js emits from
`MonacoFacade.scala`'s `@JSImport("monaco-editor", JSImport.Namespace)`.

## Why this exists

The Laminar playground module (`krueger.webapp`) exports `Main.mount` via
`@JSExportTopLevel`. That keeps `Main` reachable under the Scala.js linker's
DCE, which in turn keeps `MonacoFacade.Monaco` reachable, which makes the
linker emit a top-of-file:

```js
import * as $i_monaco$002deditor from "monaco-editor";
```

in the test bundle `out/krueger/webapp/test/fastLinkJSTest.dest/main.js`. Node
evaluates that import before any test body runs, so if the module is not
resolvable the test process exits with `ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND` before a single
assertion executes.

## Why we ship a stub instead of installing the real package

- No test actually calls into Monaco at runtime. `MonacoPlan` is a pure case
  class; `MonacoEditor.apply` is only reached from the browser.
- The real `monaco-editor` is a heavy dep whose only legitimate consumer is the
  Astro `docs/` site, where Vite bundles it properly.
- Adding a root-level `package.json` + `npm install monaco-editor` purely for
  the test runtime would bloat `node_modules/` by tens of MB and make CI slower.

A ~10-line ES-module stub with empty `editor` / `languages` namespaces is
enough to satisfy Node's resolver. Tests still exercise the pure logic; the
browser pulls the real package via Vite.

## When to delete this

If we ever switch `MonacoFacade` away from `@JSImport` (e.g., to a dynamic
`import('monaco-editor')` inside the mount callback, or to a `js.Dynamic.global`
lookup), the top-level import disappears from the test bundle and this stub is
no longer needed. Delete the directory and remove the `!/node_modules/monaco-editor/`
allowlist from the root `.gitignore`.
